use_case: Route research requests to tools for literature search, RAG retrieval, synthetic evaluation question generation, evaluation logging, and next-action planning

call_pattern.reason: Research workflows usually need retrieval before generation and evaluation after generation.

tool_schemas.name: search_research_records
tool_schemas.description: Search structured research records by topic, crop, method, and context.
tool_schemas.human_review_required: Yes before citing or using in manuscripts.

tool_schemas.name: generate_research_question
tool_schemas.description: Generate focused research questions and working titles.

tool_schemas.name: retrieve_rag_context
tool_schemas.description: Retrieve relevant chunks from the ResearchLab knowledge base.

tool_schemas.name: log_rag_evaluation
tool_schemas.description: Log query, retrieved chunks, model answer, and evaluation scores.

tool_schemas.name: generate_synthetic_eval_questions
tool_schemas.description: Generate synthetic evaluation questions for testing RAG, function calling, or model behavior.
tool_schemas.human_review_required: Yes before using in formal evaluation.

tool_schemas.name: create_next_action_plan
tool_schemas.description: Create a structured next-action plan for a user goal.
tool_schemas.human_review_required: No, unless used for high-impact decisions.

tool_schemas.name: draft_deadline_reminder
tool_schemas.description: Draft a professional reminder for upcoming deadlines.

tool_schemas.name: brave_search
tool_schemas.description: Search the web for current information when up-to-date data is required.
tool_schemas.human_review_required: Yes before citing or using externally.

tool_schemas.name: wolfram_alpha
tool_schemas.description: Run structured mathematical or computational queries.

tool_schemas.name: generate_source_attribution
tool_schemas.description: User-defined tool. Add purpose and schema before implementation.

tool_schemas.name: benchmark_llama_outputs
tool_schemas.description: User-defined tool. Add purpose and schema before implementation.

safety_rules: Reject unknown or malformed tool calls.
Do not allow the model to execute shell commands directly.
Separate tool selection, execution, and final response generation.
Log every tool call, arguments, result, timestamp, and user goal.
Require human review for manuscript, grant, farmer-facing, legal, financial, or live-trading outputs.
For web search, cite sources and check recency.
For synthetic data, label it clearly as synthetic and review before training or evaluation use.
Do not expose API keys, credentials, private files, student data, or unpublished research notes.
Add prompt injection tests before connecting real tools.
implementation_steps: Write JSON schemas for each tool.
Create a prompt that asks Llama to choose exactly one valid tool or no tool.
Validate arguments against the expected schema.
Generate a final response based only on tool result and allowed context.
Log the full interaction for evaluation.
Add human approval gates for high-risk outputs.
Test malformed tool calls and prompt injection attempts.
Compare Llama 3.1 70B and 405B on the same function-calling test set.
